content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

April 30, 2014


Ajay Shah
Associate
Olivarez Madruga, P.C.
1100 S Flower Street, Suite 2200
Los Angeles, CA 90015

Re:     Your Request for Advice
        Our File No. W-14-077

Dear Mr. Shah:

     This correspondence is written confirmation that the Fair Political Practices Commission has withdrawn your April 24, 2014, request for formal advice under the provisions of the Political Reform Act.¹ As you approved by telephone on April 29, 2014, we have provided email assistance in lieu of formal advice. Consequently, no formal response will be prepared, and your request for formal advice is withdrawn.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Zackery P. Morazzini
General Counsel

By:     Brian G. Lau
        Counsel, Legal Division

BGL:jgl

_________________________________

¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

sections.question_synthetic: The requester submitted a formal advice request on April 24, 2014, regarding an unspecified matter under the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC withdrew the formal advice request at the requester's approval, as email assistance was provided instead of formal advice.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a withdrawal letter. No QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections are present. The document confirms informal email assistance was provided instead of pursuing formal advice. File number W-14-077 indicates withdrawal status.
